reasoning.summary: Parser recovery indicates that a `let` binding was not terminated with `;`.
evidence: Compiler message: expected ';' after let binding
E1033 is emitted when the parser recovers from a missing `;` after a let binding.
The compiler produced a safe fix that inserts a semicolon at the binding tail.

hypotheses.title: Semicolon omitted after the binding expression
hypotheses.detail: The parser advanced past the let-binding expression and could not find the required statement terminator.

hypotheses.title: Later parser errors may be secondary recovery noise
hypotheses.detail: After the missing `;`, later parse spans can shift until the parser resynchronizes.

next_actions: Re-run `aic check --json` after the syntax repair to discard recovery noise.
fix the token sequence around the highlighted span
prefer building incrementally and re-running `aic check`
